title: Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
stakeholder_needs:
Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
    cleaner for home use. It should run for at least 20 minutes,
    weigh under 1.5 kg, and meet EU safety standards.
analysis: Stakeholders: (1) End users want lightweight, convenient cleaning with adequate runtime, strong suction, low noise, easy emptying, and reliable charging. (2) Safety/compliance stakeholders (EU regulators, notified bodies, retailers) need conformity to CE requirements: Low Voltage Directive (if applicable to charger), EMC, RoHS, REACH, Battery Regulation, and relevant harmonized standards (e.g., EN/IEC 60335-1 & -2-2 for household vacuum cleaners; EN 62233 for EMF; IEC 62133/UL-equivalent for Li-ion cell safety; UN 38.3 for transport; IEC 62368-1/60950-1 may apply to external power supplies; EcoDesign/Energy labeling not typical for handheld vacuums but check). (3) Manufacturer/operations need cost targets, manufacturability, durability, serviceability, supply chain stability, and testability. (4) Retailers/customer support want low return rates, clear instructions, spare parts/consumables availability, and robust packaging. Constraints already given: runtime >=20 min, mass <1.5 kg (clarify with/without accessories), EU safety compliance. Functional requirements should cover cleaning performance across common household debris, airflow/suction targets, bin volume, filtration, attachments, charging behavior, indicators, and maintenance. Non-functional requirements: reliability (cycle life), battery life degradation, noise, ergonomics, materials, drop/ingress protection, temperature limits, and compliance documentation. Verification should specify test conditions for runtime, weight, performance, and compliance testing.
clarification_questions: What floor types and cleaning scenarios are in scope (e.g., hard floors only vs carpets, pet hair, car use), and are attachments (crevice tool/brush) required?
For the 20-minute runtime requirement, what operating mode and test conditions should be used (standard vs max power, with/without brush motor, full vs empty bin, new battery vs end-of-life)?

Is the <1.5 kg weight limit for the main unit only or including battery and standard accessories, and do you have target price/noise/charging method constraints (USB-C vs dock, replaceable battery vs fixed)?

Functional requirements should cover cleaning performance across common household debris, airflow/suction targets, bin volume, filtration, attachments, charging behavior, indicators, and maintenance. Generate requirements for a battery-powered handheld vacuum
    cleaner for home use. It should run for at least 20 minutes,
    weigh under 1.5 kg, and meet EU safety standards.

Stakeholders: (1) End users want lightweight, convenient cleaning with adequate runtime, strong suction, low noise, easy emptying, and reliable charging. (2) Safety/compliance stakeholders (EU regulators, notified bodies, retailers) need conformity to CE requirements: Low Voltage Directive (if applicable to charger), EMC, RoHS, REACH, Battery Regulation, and relevant harmonized standards (e.g., EN/IEC 60335-1 & -2-2 for household vacuum cleaners; EN 62233 for EMF; IEC 62133/UL-equivalent for Li-ion cell safety; UN 38.3 for transport; IEC 62368-1/60950-1 may apply to external power supplies; EcoDesign/Energy labeling not typical for handheld vacuums but check). (3) Manufacturer/operations need cost targets, manufacturability, durability, serviceability, supply chain stability, and testability. (4) Retailers/customer support want low return rates, clear instructions, spare parts/consumables availability, and robust packaging. Constraints already given: runtime >=20 min, mass <1.5 kg (clarify with/without accessories), EU safety compliance. Functional requirements should cover cleaning performance across common household debris, airflow/suction targets, bin volume, filtration, attachments, charging behavior, indicators, and maintenance. Non-functional requirements: reliability (cycle life), battery life degradation, noise, ergonomics, materials, drop/ingress protection, temperature limits, and compliance documentation. Verification should specify test conditions for runtime, weight, performance, and compliance testing.
